Name: ART_CD4_at_Initiation_Saturating_Reduction_in_Mortality
Description: The duration from ART enrollment to on-ART HIV-cause death increases with CD4 at ART initiation up to a threshold determined by this parameter value. This is the maximum value that CD4 is allowed to have in the hazard ratio calculation for CD4.

Name: ART_Is_Active_Against_Mortality_And_Transmission
Description: If set to true (1), ART will suppress viral load and extend prognosis.

Name: ART_Multiplier_On_Transmission_Prob_Per_Act
Description: Multiplier acting on Base_Infectivity to determine the per-act transmission probability of a virally suppressed HIV+ individual.

Name: ART_Survival_Baseline_Hazard_Weibull_Scale
Description: Scale parameter for a Weibull distribution of survival time in years for a male < 40 with WHO stage of 1 or 2 starting ART (base case).

Name: ART_Survival_Baseline_Hazard_Weibull_Shape
Description: Shape parameter for a Weibull distribution of survival time in years for a male < 40 with WHO stage of 1 or 2 starting ART (base case).

Name: ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_Age_Over_40Yr
Description: The hazard ratio comparing the survival time of those starting ART over 40 years of age compared to those starting ART <40 years.

Name: ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_Body_Weight_Kg_Intercept
Description:
The Y-intercept to use when calculating the hazard ratio for the person's body weight.

The body weight is determined by WHO stage:

* WHO Stage 0 = 65.0 kg
* WHO Stage 1-2 = 62.1 kg
* WHO Stage 2-3 = 57.0 kg
* WHO Stage 3-4 = 50.0 kg
* WHO Stage 4+ = 40.1 kg

multiplier = exp(weight_slope * weight + weight_intercept)

Name: ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_Body_Weight_Kg_Slope
Description:
The slope to use when calculating the hazard ratio for the person's body weight.

The body weight is determined by WHO stage:

* WHO Stage 0 = 65.0 kg
* WHO Stage 1-2 = 62.1 kg
* WHO Stage 2-3 = 57.0 kg
* WHO Stage 3-4 = 50.0 kg
* WHO Stage 4+ = 40.1 kg

multiplier = exp(weight_slope * weight + weight_intercept)

Name: ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_CD4_Intercept
Description:
The Y-intercept to use when calculating the hazard ratio for the person based on their CD4 count.

multiplier = exp(cd4_slope * cd4 + cd4_intercept)

Name: ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_CD4_Slope
Description:
The slope value to sue when calculating the hazard for for the person based on their CD4 count.

multiplier = exp(cd4_slope * cd4 + cd4_intercept)

Name: ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_Female
Description: The hazard ratio comparing survival female to male survival for those starting ART.

Name: ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_WHO_Stage_3Plus
Description: The hazard ratio comparing those starting ART in WHO stage >= 3 to those in WHO stage < 3.

Name: ART_Survival_WHO_Stage_Threshold_For_Cox
Description: If the person receiving ART has a WHO Stage greater than or equal to this threshold, then use the hazard ratio determined by the parameter **ART_Survival_Hazard_Ratio_WHO_Stage_3Plus**.

Name: Cost_To_Consumer
Description: Unit cost per drug (unamortized).

Name: Days_To_Achieve_Viral_Suppression
Description: The number of days after ART initiation over which infectiousness declines linearly until the **ART_Multiplier_On_Transmission_Prob_Per_Act** takes full effect.

Name: Disqualifying_Properties
Description: A list of **IndividualProperty** key:value pairs that cause an intervention to be aborted (persistent interventions will stop being distributed to individuals with these values). See :ref:`demo-properties` parameters for more information. Generally used to control the flow of health care access. For example, to prevent the same individual from accessing health care via two different routes at the same time.

Name: Dont_Allow_Duplicates
Description: If an individual's container has an intervention, set to true (1) to prevent them from receiving another copy of the intervention. Supported by all intervention classes.

Name: Intervention_Name
Description: The optional name used to refer to this intervention as a means to differentiate it from others that use the same class.

Name: New_Property_Value
Description: An optional **IndividualProperty** key:value pair that will be assigned when the intervention is distributed. See :ref:`demo-properties` parameters for more information. Generally used to indicate the broad category of health care cascade to which an intervention belongs to prevent individuals from accessing care through multiple pathways. For example, if an individual must already be taking a particular medication to be prescribed a new one.

Name: Stop_ART_Event
Description: This event is broadcasted when the person drops off ART. This could happen either via the timer running out or the intervention detected a disqualifying property.  See :doc:`parameter-campaign-event-list` for possible values or define custom events by adding to the list for the configuration parameter **Custom_Individual_Event**.

Name: Time_On_ART_Constant
Description: Each instance will receive this constant/fixed value.

Name: Time_On_ART_Distribution
Description:
The type of distribution to use when determine how long a person will be on ART. Possible values are:

* NOT_INITIALIZED
* CONSTANT_DISTRIBUTION
* UNIFORM_DISTRIBUTION
* GAUSSIAN_DISTRIBUTION
* EXPONENTIAL_DISTRIBUTION
* POISSON_DISTRIBUTION
* LOG_NORMAL_DISTRIBUTION
* DUAL_CONSTANT_DISTRIBUTION
* WEIBULL_DISTRIBUTION
* DUAL_EXPONENTIAL_DISTRIBUTION

Name: Time_On_ART_Exponential
Description: The mean for an exponential distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Gaussian_Mean
Description: The mean for a Gaussian distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Gaussian_Std_Dev
Description: The standard deviation for a Gaussian distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Kappa
Description: The shape value in a Weibull distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Lambda
Description: The scale value in a Weibull distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Log_Normal_Mu
Description: The mean for a log-normal distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Log_Normal_Sigma
Description: The width for a log-normal distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Max
Description: The maximum of the uniform distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Mean_1
Description: The mean of the first exponential distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Mean_2
Description: The mean of the second exponential distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Min
Description: The minimum of the uniform distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Peak_2_Value
Description: The value to assign to the remaining individuals.

Name: Time_On_ART_Poisson_Mean
Description: The mean for a Poisson distribution.

Name: Time_On_ART_Proportion_0
Description: The proportion of individuals to assign a value of zero.

Name: Time_On_ART_Proportion_1
Description: The proportion of individuals in the first exponential distribution.
